name: xsv (Fast CSV Toolkit)

performance.notes: Rust; processes GB/s; massively faster than Python alternatives; indexed mode for random access

agent_readiness.notes: High-performance CSV toolkit written in Rust. Commands: count, headers, select, slice, search, frequency, stats, sort, join, index, split, sample, flatten, fmt, cat. Built-in indexing for fast random access and slicing. Handles gigabyte-scale CSVs efficiently. Commands composable via pipes. Stats command gives descriptive statistics. Join on multiple columns. Sampling for large datasets. Successor: qsv (more features, actively maintained fork).

gotchas: No longer actively maintained (use qsv fork for new features)
No SQL support (use csvsql from csvkit)
Limited compared to DuckDB for complex queries
No built-in Excel import (use csvkit's in2csv first)
best_when: Processing large CSV files (> 100MB) where Python tools are slow
Fast column selection, filtering, and stats on large datasets
Sampling or slicing huge CSVs for downstream analysis
avoid_when: SQL queries on CSV (use DuckDB or csvsql)
Active development needing new features (use qsv instead)
summary: xsv is a high-performance Rust CSV toolkit by BurntSushi (ripgrep author). Processes gigabyte-scale CSV files at GB/s speeds. Commands: select, slice, search, stats, sort, join, frequency, sample, index. Far faster than Python alternatives for large files. No longer actively maintained — use qsv (actively maintained fork with more features). Essential for large CSV processing in Unix pipelines.
